title: Dryopteris navarrensis
intro: Dryopteris navarrensis, also known as Navarro Male Fern, is a species of fern native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris navarrensis include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dryopteris navarrensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from fever and inflammation to digestive problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Dryopteris navarrensis is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This genus is a part of the larger division of Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns. The species is further divided into the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all ferns with vascular tissues and true leaves. Dryopteris navarrensis is also a member of the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns that have highly divided leaves. This classification indicates the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
